BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow will fall on Saturday in some localities. As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

During the day: On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions over a wide area 40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.
In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Significant increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: On wind-loaded slopes a dangerous avalanche situation will persist. The danger of dry and moist avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and, in many cases, large powder avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions 70 to 120 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1800 m. Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar in some places above the tree line. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.
Performed ECTs and field observations have confirmed the existence of a weak snowack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Significant increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs require caution. As a consequence of the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and moist avalanches being released will increase in particular on sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Thursday in all altitude zones. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
Early morning: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
On sunny slopes and in steep rocky terrain the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant above the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Thursday in all altitude zones. In these regions above approximately 1200 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
